User Story
As an exporter

I need to be able to select transport mode

So that my document complies with EU regulations

Acceptance Criteria
Scenario 1 - Rename title and make label text bold

Given I have navigated to the page /how-does-the-export-leave-the-uk in my non-manipulation document user journey

When the page loads

Then the title is renamed from ‘How does the export leave the UK?’ to ‘How do you transport the consignment out of the UK?’

And the text ‘Select a type of transport’ is changed to ‘Select the type of transport’ and is bold

And the guidance text underneath ‘Truck’ is ‘Select truck if your vehicle travels by ferry or through the Eurotunnel.’

 

Scenario 2 - Welsh translations

Given I have navigated to the page /how-does-the-export-leave-the-uk in my non-manipulation document user journey

When I have selected Welsh language

Then the guidance text is translated to Welsh

English text

Welsh translation

How do you transport the consignment out of the UK?

Sut rydych chi'n cludo'r llwyth allan o'r DU?

Select the type of transport

Dewiswch y math o gludiant.

Select truck if your vehicle travels by ferry or through the Eurotunnel.

Dewiswch lori os yw eich cerbyd yn teithio ar fferi neu drwy'r Eurotunnel.

Scenario 3 - Non-JS

Given I have navigated to the page /how-does-the-export-leave-the-uk in my non-manipulation document user journey

When javascript is turned off

Then all the page must be compliant with non javascript

 

Scenario 4 -  Accessibility
Given I have navigated to the page /how-does-the-export-leave-the-uk in my non-manipulation document user journey

Then the page must comply with GDS accessibility guidelines

And all fields, guidance text, and buttons are keyboard accessible

And screen reader compatible
